_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development/eval.

procedure.name: EGD (esophagogastroduodenoscopy) + Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 9.6, ferritin 11) with new melena — evaluate for GI bleeding source
procedure.sedation: General anesthesia with endotracheal intubation, anesthesiologist-directed. Difficult-airway precautions: awake video laryngoscopy (GlideScope) with bougie, given documented Cormack-Lehane grade 3 (2022), untreated severe OSA, and class III obesity (BMI 54). Weekly semaglutide held 3 weeks pre-procedure; extended clear-liquid diet and prolonged fast; point-of-care gastric ultrasound day-of showed empty antrum. Airway secured on first attempt with video laryngoscopy.
procedure.prep_quality: Colonoscopy: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 7/9 (right 2, transverse 2, left 3) — adequate
procedure.extent_reached: EGD: second/third portion of duodenum. Colonoscopy: cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: EGD followed by colonoscopy under general anesthesia with difficult-airway precautions. INDICATION: iron-deficiency anemia with new melena. EGD FINDINGS: The esophagus was normal with a normal Z-line; no varices. In the gastric antrum a single 15 mm clean-based to fibrin-covered ulcer was identified along the lesser curvature with an adherent clot; after clot washout a non-bleeding visible vessel (Forrest IIa) was seen. Hemostasis achieved with epinephrine injection (1:10,000) and placement of two through-the-scope hemoclips; no active bleeding on completion. The ulcer margins were irregular and were biopsied (4 samples) to exclude malignancy. Gastric body/antral mucosal biopsies obtained for H. pylori. The duodenal bulb and second/third portions were normal. COLONOSCOPY FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum; terminal ileum normal. A single 6 mm sessile polyp in the ascending colon was removed by cold snare and retrieved. Scattered non-bleeding diverticula in the sigmoid. No colonic source of bleeding or angioectasia identified. IMPRESSION: 15 mm antral gastric ulcer with non-bleeding visible vessel (Forrest IIa) — endoscopic hemostasis performed; ulcer biopsied to exclude malignancy. H. pylori testing pending. 6 mm ascending colon polyp removed. Diverticulosis. Melena attributed to the gastric ulcer. Pathology pending.

findings.finding: 15 mm antral gastric ulcer, lesser curvature, non-bleeding visible vessel (Forrest IIa), irregular margins
findings.action_taken: epinephrine injection + 2 hemoclips (hemostasis achieved); margin biopsies x4 to exclude malignancy

findings.finding: Gastric mucosal biopsies for H. pylori

disposition: Extubated awake without event; monitored in PACU with extended observation given untreated OSA and class III obesity. Admitted overnight for observation of the recently bleeding gastric ulcer. Hemodynamically stable, Hgb stable. Started on high-dose IV proton pump inhibitor (pantoprazole 80 mg bolus then infusion), transitioning to twice-daily oral PPI. Post-hemostasis and post-polypectomy precautions reviewed.

followup.recommendation: Review gastric ulcer margin biopsy pathology (malignancy exclusion) — communicate result and act on it; ulcer margins were irregular
Review H. pylori biopsy result; if positive, treat with eradication therapy and confirm eradication with post-treatment testing
Review ascending colon polyp pathology and set colon surveillance interval
Repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm gastric ulcer healing and re-biopsy if not fully healed (mandatory for gastric ulcer)
Continue twice-daily PPI; monitor for rebleeding (hematemesis, recurrent melena, lightheadedness); recheck CBC
Resume weekly semaglutide (Wegovy) only after recovery per GI/anesthesia guidance; do not resume during acute ulcer-bleed observation window
Continue oral iron for iron-deficiency anemia; recheck Hgb/ferritin to confirm response now that bleeding source is treated

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric antral ulcer margin biopsies pending to exclude malignancy; margins were irregular. Result must be reviewed and communicated regardless of endoscopic hemostasis.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path within 1 week; if dysplasia/malignancy, urgent oncology/surgical referral and staging; if benign, ensure healing follow-up

planted_followup_items.description: Mandatory repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to document gastric ulcer healing and re-biopsy if unhealed — standard of care for any gastric ulcer to exclude occult malignancy.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule surveillance EGD with anesthesiology-directed difficult-airway plan

planted_followup_items.description: Forrest IIa gastric ulcer with non-bleeding visible vessel post-hemostasis — rebleeding risk in first 72 hours from the endoscopic hemostasis performed at this procedure. Continue high-dose PPI; monitor for hematemesis/recurrent melena and hemoglobin drop.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: inpatient observation, serial CBC, PPI; re-scope if rebleeding

planted_followup_items.description: H. pylori biopsy result pending — if positive requires eradication therapy and post-treatment confirmation of eradication.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review result; treat and confirm eradication if positive

planted_followup_items.description: Ascending colon polypectomy pathology pending — drives colon surveillance interval.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path; document surveillance interval per histology/size

planted_followup_items.description: Weekly semaglutide (Wegovy) was held 3 weeks pre-procedure (GLP-1, not an anticoagulant/antiplatelet) and remains held during acute ulcer-bleed observation; resumption timing must be actively decided and communicated.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm safe resumption timing with patient after recovery; reconcile on active med list

planted_followup_items.description: Bleeding source for iron-deficiency anemia identified and treated (gastric ulcer). Anemia workup loop must be closed: recheck Hgb/ferritin to confirm response and continue iron.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: repeat CBC/iron studies in 4-6 weeks; escalate if no response

planted_followup_items.description: Incidental but active safety issue surfaced at pre-procedure: untreated severe OSA (AHI 42, CPAP declined) plus class III obesity and known difficult airway. Relevant to any future sedation/anesthesia and overall health.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: re-refer to sleep medicine / reinforce OSA treatment; keep difficult-airway flag active

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. Synthetic; benign_only profile (uneventful recovery).

contacts.verbatim: My throat's kind of raw and I feel really gassy and bloated. Is that normal after all this?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-intubation/EGD sore throat plus colonoscopy insufflation bloating; no bleeding or airway symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat (intubation/scope) and gas/bloating (insufflation) are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Warm fluids, walking. Review return precautions for vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, dizziness.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy/anesthesia symptoms; no red flag despite the recent ulcer, since no bleeding or hemodynamic signal.

contacts.verbatim: The hospital told me to skip my weekly Wegovy shot before the scope. When am I allowed to take it again? And any news on the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about resuming held semaglutide and status of pending malignancy-exclusion/H. pylori pathology
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: continue holding weekly semaglutide during the acute ulcer-bleed recovery window; GI/PCP will confirm the exact restart date after recovery. Advise pathology is pending (expected ~2026-08-11); the team will call with the ulcer-margin and H. pylori results and next steps.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the record; maps to the med_reconciliation item (F6) for semaglutide restart timing, with pathology status (F1/F4) addressed informationally.

contacts.verbatim: Can I go back to my regular diet now, and should I keep taking the iron pills you started me on?
contacts.clinical_signal: Diet advancement question and confirmation to continue oral iron for iron-deficiency anemia workup
contacts.expected_action: Advise gradual return to regular diet as tolerated; continue twice-daily PPI and continue oral iron as directed. Recheck Hgb/ferritin in 4-6 weeks to confirm response. Reinforce return precautions for hematemesis, recurrent melena, or lightheadedness.
contacts.rationale: Answerable self-care/plan question; links to the anemia lab-follow-up item (F7). No warning features reported.
